summary: Unconjugated bilirubin levels are elevated in conditions like Crigler-Najjar and hemolysis where there’s impaired or insufficient conjugation, whereas they remain normal in Dubin-Johnson syndrome which only impairs excretion. \(\blacksquare\)
formatted_summary: agent2 (Round 1 summarized): Unconjugated bilirubin levels are elevated in conditions like Crigler-Najjar and hemolysis where there’s impaired or insufficient conjugation, whereas they remain normal in Dubin-Johnson syndrome which only impairs excretion. \(\blacksquare\). Answer choice: B
